summary: Small cleanup PR for SysDolphin baselib list and memory code. It removed an extra inline singly-linked-list allocator wrapper, routed callers through HSD_SListAlloc, converted repeated manual __assert calls to the assert_line macro, normalized local includes to same-directory headers, and made minor style/name cleanups in HSD_MemAlloc.
what_changed: In src/sysdolphin/baselib/list.c, changed the header include from "sysdolphin/baselib/list.h" to the local include "list.h".
Removed the static inline helper HSD_SListAlloc_inline, which duplicated HSD_SListAlloc.
Changed HSD_SListAllocAndAppend and HSD_SListAllocAndPrepend to call HSD_SListAlloc directly instead of the removed inline wrapper.
Replaced manual null-check assertions using __assert(__FILE__, line, "name") with assert_line(line, expr) in HSD_SListAlloc, HSD_SListAppendList, and HSD_SListPrependList.
In src/sysdolphin/baselib/memory.c, changed the header include from "sysdolphin/baselib/memory.h" to the local include "memory.h".
Reformatted HSD_MemAlloc to project brace style and renamed the local allocation result variable from result to adr.
Replaced HSD_MemAlloc's manual __assert("memory.c", 52, "adr") with assert_line(52, adr).
smart_moves: Kept the original assertion line numbers when converting to assert_line, preserving the decompiled source's intended assert metadata.
Removed duplicated allocation logic instead of maintaining a separate inline allocator that did the same HSD_ObjAlloc, assert, memset, and return sequence.
Used the variable name adr in HSD_MemAlloc, matching the assertion string previously used by the original manual __assert call.
Reduced noisy boilerplate while leaving behavior-equivalent allocation, null handling, and list-linking logic intact.
decomp_lessons: When an assert_line macro exists, prefer it over spelling out __assert with __FILE__ or a literal filename, as long as the original line number and asserted expression are preserved.
Avoid retaining static inline helper functions that only duplicate a public function unless there is matching evidence that the inline body is required for codegen.
For baselib source files, local headers can be included with same-directory paths such as "list.h" and "memory.h" rather than longer project-relative paths when consistent with the file layout.
Local variable names can matter for decomp readability and assertion fidelity; here adr was a better fit than result because the original assert text was "adr".
Cleanup PRs in matched decomp code should minimize semantic movement: this patch mostly collapsed duplicate code and macroized asserts without changing the core control flow.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_SListAlloc for the canonical singly-linked-list allocation routine; do not introduce or retain HSD_SListAlloc_inline without evidence.
Use adr as the local pointer name in HSD_MemAlloc when matching the allocation/assert pattern shown here.
Use assert_line(line, expression) for assertions in these baselib files rather than direct __assert calls.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserve original assert line constants such as 76, 179, 202, and 52 when replacing manual __assert calls with assert_line.
Removing an extraneous inline function can be a safe cleanup when all call sites can use the non-inline function and no diff evidence indicates the inline body is required for matching.
Consolidating duplicated allocator code through HSD_SListAlloc reduces the number of places where memset and allocation assertion behavior must be kept in sync.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explicitly described the intent as removing an extraneous inline function and simplifying asserts with the assert_line macro.
follow_up_queries: Find other baselib files still using manual __assert calls that could be converted to assert_line while preserving line numbers.
Search for remaining duplicate inline wrappers around allocation routines in sysdolphin baselib.
Check whether other source files include sysdolphin/baselib/*.h from the same directory and can be normalized to local includes.
Search HSD_MemAlloc call sites and related memory functions for consistent adr naming and assert_line usage.
